title: Neodryas weberbaueriana

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Neodryas weberbaueriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as dried extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Neodryas weberbaueriana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Neodryas and is a type of flowering plant native to the Andes mountain range in Peru. The classification of Neodryas weberbaueriana places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Its classification is part of the larger system of plant classification known as the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group (APG) system.
